examples.sentence: Since the bus was canceled, I had no choice but to go to work on shanks' pony this morning.
examples.translation: Because the bus was canceled, I had to walk to work this morning.

examples.sentence: Whenever I visit the park, I prefer using shanks' pony to soak in the fresh air and avoid traffic.
examples.translation: Whenever I visit the park, I prefer walking to enjoy the fresh air and avoid traffic.

examples.sentence: We can't afford a taxi, so we'll have to rely on shanks' pony for the rest of the day.
examples.translation: We can't afford a taxi, so we'll have to walk for the rest of the day.

examples.sentence: After the car broke down, Sarah got around town on shanks' pony until she could get it fixed.
examples.translation: After the car broke down, Sarah traveled on foot around town until she could get it fixed.

examples.sentence: Our hiking trip was a solid shanks' pony adventure, covering miles through the forest trails.
examples.translation: Our hiking trip was a journey on foot, covering miles through the forest trails.

examples.sentence: Instead of driving, they decided to take a shanks' pony to explore the city streets more intimately.
examples.translation: Instead of driving, they chose to walk to explore the city streets more closely.

examples.sentence: After dinner, we went for a shanks' pony around the neighborhood to help digest the meal.
examples.translation: After dinner, we took a walk around the neighborhood to help digest the meal.

examples.sentence: If you want to stay on the team, you'll need to shape up your attitude during practice.
examples.translation: If you want to stay on the team, you must improve your attitude during practice.

examples.sentence: The manager told Kevin that his work had to shape up or he'd risk losing his promotion.
examples.translation: The manager told Kevin that his work needed to improve or he might lose his promotion.

examples.sentence: After several warnings, Lisa finally shaped up and started meeting all her deadlines.
examples.translation: After several warnings, Lisa improved her behavior and began meeting all her deadlines.

examples.sentence: The new project is starting to shape up nicely, and the team is optimistic about the results.
examples.translation: The new project is beginning to develop well, and the team feels hopeful about the results.

examples.sentence: Although the first draft was rough, the report shaped up better after the revisions.
examples.translation: Although the first draft was rough, the report took a clearer form after the revisions.

examples.sentence: The event didn't shape up as expected, but we managed to adapt and make it successful.
examples.translation: The event did not develop as expected, but we adapted and made it successful.

examples.sentence: After the supervisor's warning, the staff shaped up quickly to meet the new safety standards.
examples.translation: After the supervisor's warning, the staff conformed quickly to meet the new safety standards.

examples.sentence: You better shape up or the company might decide to replace you with someone more reliable.
examples.translation: You should conform to expectations or the company might replace you with someone more reliable.

examples.sentence: The apprentice shaped up after some tough feedback, impressing the master craftsman.
examples.translation: The apprentice conformed to the required standards after receiving tough feedback, impressing the master craftsman.

examples.sentence: Tom shapes up every morning by jogging and doing exercises to stay healthy.
examples.translation: Tom improves his physical condition every morning by jogging and exercising to stay healthy.

examples.sentence: She is shaping up nicely after joining the new training program, showing great progress.
examples.translation: She is improving well after joining the new training program, showing great progress.

examples.sentence: You should shape up your presentation before the meeting to impress the clients.
examples.translation: You should improve your presentation before the meeting to impress the clients.

examples.sentence: If he doesn't shape up soon, he'll struggle to keep up with the rest of the team.
examples.translation: If he doesn't improve soon, he will have difficulty keeping up with the rest of the team.

examples.sentence: The coach gave the players a clear message: shape up or ship out before the season starts.
examples.translation: The coach told the players to improve their performance or leave before the season starts.

examples.sentence: If the interns don't shape up or ship out, the company won't hesitate to terminate their contracts.
examples.translation: If the interns don't improve or leave, the company will terminate their contracts.

examples.sentence: After several complaints, the staff finally realized they had to shape up or ship out to keep their jobs.
examples.translation: After several complaints, the staff understood they had to improve or leave to keep their jobs.

examples.sentence: The manager's ultimatum was clear: shape up or ship out, no exceptions.
examples.translation: The manager demanded compliance: improve or leave, no exceptions.

examples.sentence: When the team started slacking, the leader reminded everyone to shape up or ship out immediately.
examples.translation: When the team became lazy, the leader told everyone to improve or leave immediately.

examples.sentence: You will have to shape up or ship out if you want to be part of this project going forward.
examples.translation: You must improve or leave if you want to continue being part of this project.

examples.sentence: The new recruit was told to shape up or ship out after missing several deadlines.
examples.translation: The new recruit was told to improve or leave after missing several deadlines.

examples.sentence: Employees who don't shape up or ship out create problems for the whole department.
examples.translation: Employees who don't improve or leave cause problems for the entire department.

examples.sentence: Our supervisor gave us the choice: shape up or ship out by the end of the month.
examples.translation: Our supervisor told us to improve or leave by the end of the month.

examples.sentence: They have been warned to shape up or ship out several times, but some still haven't changed.
examples.translation: They have been warned to improve or leave several times, but some remain unchanged.

examples.sentence: When the siblings inherited the estate, they decided to share and share alike to avoid disputes.
examples.translation: When the siblings inherited the estate, they divided it equally to avoid disputes.

examples.sentence: In the business partnership, profits are shared and shared alike among all partners.
examples.translation: In the business partnership, profits are divided equally among all partners.

examples.sentence: Let's share and share alike the workload so no one feels overwhelmed during the event.
examples.translation: Let's divide the workload equally so no one feels overwhelmed during the event.

examples.sentence: The two friends agreed to share and share alike the responsibility for their community project.
examples.translation: The two friends agreed to take equal ownership of the responsibility for their community project.

examples.sentence: In the startup, all employees share and share alike the benefits of the company’s success.
examples.translation: In the startup, all employees equally enjoy the benefits of the company’s success.

examples.sentence: They decided to share and share alike the apartment, splitting rent and chores evenly.
examples.translation: They decided to equally share the apartment, splitting rent and chores evenly.

examples.sentence: At the picnic, everyone shared and shared alike the sandwiches and drinks brought by the group.
examples.translation: At the picnic, everyone divided the sandwiches and drinks equally among the group.

examples.sentence: The charity event organizers made sure to share and share alike all donations received.
examples.translation: The charity event organizers divided all donations equally.

examples.sentence: During the camping trip, supplies were shared and shared alike so no one ran short.
examples.translation: During the camping trip, supplies were divided equally so no one ran out.

examples.sentence: They shared and shared alike the profits from their joint venture without any complaints.
examples.translation: They divided the profits from their joint venture equally without complaints.

examples.sentence: Don't underestimate Jane; she's a sharp cookie who quickly figures out complex problems.
examples.translation: Don't underestimate Jane; she's an intelligent person who quickly understands complex problems.

examples.sentence: Mark proved to be a sharp cookie by identifying the flaws in the proposal right away.
examples.translation: Mark showed his intelligence by quickly spotting the flaws in the proposal.

examples.sentence: You need a sharp cookie like Sam to manage this project, given its complexity and deadlines.
examples.translation: You need a smart and quick-witted person like Sam to manage this complex project.

examples.sentence: The detective was a sharp cookie, always noticing details others missed at the crime scene.
examples.translation: The detective was very astute, always noticing details others overlooked at the crime scene.

examples.sentence: She's a sharp cookie on the streets, knowing how to handle tricky situations skillfully.
examples.translation: She is very shrewd and practical, able to handle difficult situations well.

examples.sentence: Only a sharp cookie could negotiate such a favorable deal under those conditions.
examples.translation: Only a clever and shrewd person could get such a good deal in those circumstances.

examples.sentence: Everyone agrees that Tom is a sharp cookie when it comes to solving math puzzles.
examples.translation: Everyone agrees that Tom is an intelligent and quick-witted person in solving math puzzles.

examples.sentence: She’s a sharp cookie who quickly adapts to new challenges at work.
examples.translation: She is an intelligent person who quickly adjusts to new challenges at work.

examples.sentence: Being a sharp cookie, he easily grasps complicated instructions without needing help.
examples.translation: Being intelligent and quick-witted, he understands complicated instructions easily.

examples.sentence: The team hired a sharp cookie to lead the innovation department due to her quick thinking.
examples.translation: The team hired an intelligent and quick-witted person to lead the innovation department.

examples.sentence: After skipping lunch, I was sharp-set enough to eat an entire pizza by dinner.
examples.translation: After skipping lunch, I was very hungry and ate an entire pizza by dinner.

examples.sentence: The campers returned from their long hike feeling sharp-set and ready for a big meal.
examples.translation: The campers came back from their long hike very hungry and ready to eat a lot.

examples.sentence: Whenever I get sharp-set, I crave something hearty and filling like stew or pasta.
examples.translation: Whenever I am very hungry, I want something hearty like stew or pasta.

examples.sentence: She was so sharp-set after the workout that she ate double her usual portion.
examples.translation: She was very hungry after the workout and ate twice her usual amount.

examples.sentence: The young artist was sharp-set to begin her career, eager and full of energy.
examples.translation: The young artist was intensely eager to start her career, full of energy.

examples.sentence: Despite the challenges, he remained sharp-set and committed to finishing the project.
examples.translation: Despite difficulties, he stayed intensely eager and committed to completing the project.

examples.sentence: They were sharp-set to prove everyone wrong and worked tirelessly to succeed.
examples.translation: They were intensely eager to prove others wrong and worked hard to succeed.

examples.sentence: After skipping breakfast, I was sharp-set and couldn't wait to eat lunch.
examples.translation: After skipping breakfast, I was very hungry and eager to eat lunch.

examples.sentence: The sharp-set puppy ate all its food within minutes of being served.
examples.translation: The very hungry puppy ate all its food quickly.

examples.sentence: She felt sharp-set after the long run and immediately looked for a snack.
examples.translation: She was very hungry after the long run and immediately wanted a snack.

examples.sentence: We were all sharp-set by the time dinner was ready, so we ate heartily.
examples.translation: We were all very hungry by dinner time and ate a lot.

examples.sentence: Be careful what you say around Lisa; she has a sharp tongue that can easily offend.
examples.translation: Be careful what you say around Lisa; she speaks in a cutting manner that can offend.

examples.sentence: John’s sharp tongue sometimes gets him into trouble with colleagues during meetings.
examples.translation: John’s habit of speaking harshly sometimes causes problems with coworkers.

examples.sentence: Despite her sharp tongue, many appreciate her honesty and directness.
examples.translation: Despite speaking bluntly, many people value her honesty.

examples.sentence: His sharp tongue made it difficult for others to accept his criticism gracefully.
examples.translation: His habit of making harsh remarks made it hard for others to take his criticism well.

examples.sentence: She often uses a sharp tongue when debating, sometimes crossing the line into rudeness.
examples.translation: She frequently speaks sharply during debates, sometimes becoming rude.

examples.sentence: The politician's sharp tongue alienated some voters but impressed others with his boldness.
examples.translation: The politician’s blunt speech upset some voters but impressed others.

examples.sentence: His sharp tongue was evident when he made a biting comment about the report.
examples.translation: He spoke in a cutting way when he made a critical comment about the report.

examples.sentence: She has a sharp tongue, which means she often speaks in a biting or sarcastic manner.
examples.translation: She tends to speak in a cutting or sarcastic way.

examples.sentence: The sharp tongue of the critic left the artist feeling discouraged and upset.
examples.translation: The critic’s harsh way of speaking made the artist feel discouraged.

examples.sentence: Having a sharp tongue can be a double-edged sword, helping you be honest but also offending others.
examples.translation: Speaking sharply can help you be honest but also hurt others’ feelings.

examples.sentence: To shatter the ice at the meeting, Maria told a funny story that got everyone laughing.
examples.translation: To break the initial tension at the meeting, Maria told a joke that made everyone laugh.

examples.sentence: It took a few awkward moments before John finally shattered the ice and started a conversation.
examples.translation: It took some awkward moments before John broke the initial reserve and began talking.

examples.sentence: The team-building games helped shatter the ice between the new members and veterans.
examples.translation: The games helped break the initial barriers between new and experienced members.

examples.sentence: Her bold question during the seminar shattered the ice, encouraging others to speak up.
examples.translation: Her bold question broke the initial reserve, encouraging others to participate.

examples.sentence: The comedian shattered the ice quickly with a sharp joke that engaged the audience immediately.
examples.translation: The comedian broke the formality quickly with a joke that got the audience involved.

examples.sentence: We used a fun activity to shatter the ice and make everyone feel comfortable at the workshop.
examples.translation: We used a fun activity to break the initial awkwardness and help everyone relax.

examples.sentence: The heavy hammer finally shattered the ice on the frozen lake, opening a clear patch of water.
examples.translation: The heavy hammer broke through the ice on the frozen lake, creating an open water area.

examples.sentence: During the winter, the workers used machinery to shatter the ice covering the roads for safe travel.
examples.translation: During winter, workers broke up the ice on the roads using machines to make travel safe.

examples.sentence: The ship’s hull was designed to shatter the ice in Arctic waters as it moved forward.
examples.translation: The ship’s hull was made to break through ice in Arctic waters while moving.

examples.sentence: We watched as the ice shattered under the weight of the falling tree branch near the riverbank.
examples.translation: We saw the ice break apart under the weight of the falling tree branch by the river.

examples.sentence: Before the summer holiday, she decided to shave down to get ready for the beach season.
examples.translation: Before the summer holiday, she shaved her body hair to prepare for the beach season.

examples.sentence: He shaved down his beard carefully to get a clean, fresh look for the interview.
examples.translation: He shaved his beard carefully to look neat for the interview.

examples.sentence: Many athletes shave down to reduce drag and improve their performance in competitions.
examples.translation: Many athletes remove body hair to reduce resistance and perform better in competitions.

examples.sentence: She has been shaving down regularly as part of her grooming routine.
examples.translation: She regularly removes body hair as part of her grooming.

examples.sentence: The carpenter shaved down the edges of the wood to make a smooth finish for the table.
examples.translation: The carpenter trimmed the wood edges to make the table surface smooth.

examples.sentence: Dentists often shave down teeth slightly before fitting crowns to ensure a proper fit.
examples.translation: Dentists reduce the thickness of teeth before placing crowns to fit properly.

examples.sentence: To fix the uneven surface, the technician shaved down the metal plate carefully.
examples.translation: To correct the uneven surface, the technician smoothed the metal plate by shaving it down.

examples.sentence: They shaved down the door frame so the new door could close properly.
examples.translation: They trimmed the door frame so the new door would close correctly.

examples.sentence: The company shaved down expenses to cope with the economic downturn.
examples.translation: The company reduced expenses to manage the economic decline.

examples.sentence: We need to shave down the budget if we want to complete the project on time.
examples.translation: We have to reduce the budget to finish the project on time.

examples.sentence: After negotiating, they managed to shave down the price to a more affordable level.
examples.translation: After negotiation, they reduced the price to make it more affordable.

examples.sentence: At the end of the duel, the knight sheathed the sword and bowed to his opponent.
examples.translation: After the duel, the knight put his sword into its sheath and bowed to his opponent.

examples.sentence: He quickly sheathed the sword when the battle was over to signal peace.
examples.translation: He put away his sword quickly after the battle finished to show peace.

examples.sentence: The samurai sheathed the sword carefully after the ceremony concluded.
examples.translation: The samurai put the sword into its scabbard carefully after the ceremony ended.

examples.sentence: After drawing his sword to defend the village, he sheathed it once the danger passed.
examples.translation: After taking out his sword to protect the village, he put it away once the threat was gone.

examples.sentence: The two countries decided to sheathe the sword and begin peace talks after years of conflict.
examples.translation: The two countries agreed to stop fighting and start peace negotiations after years of war.

examples.sentence: After the argument, they both sheathed the sword and agreed to move forward amicably.
examples.translation: After the argument, they both stopped hostility and agreed to proceed peacefully.

examples.sentence: It’s time to sheathe the sword and find a solution that benefits both sides.
examples.translation: It’s time to stop fighting and find a mutually beneficial solution.

examples.sentence: She shed a tear when she heard the emotional news about her childhood friend.
examples.translation: She cried when she heard the emotional news about her childhood friend.

examples.sentence: During the heartfelt speech, many in the audience shed tears of joy and gratitude.
examples.translation: During the emotional speech, many people cried tears of happiness and thanks.

examples.sentence: I never thought I would shed a tear at the end of the movie, but it really touched me.
examples.translation: I didn’t expect to cry at the end of the movie, but it moved me deeply.

examples.sentence: He tried not to shed a tear during the farewell, but one escaped despite his efforts.
examples.translation: He tried to hold back crying during the goodbye, but a tear came out anyway.
